signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Macro Divergence
signals.text: GBP/AUD at 1.8868 — G8 scorecard identifies a critical bilateral theme as GBP outperforms (+0.558%) while AUD collapses (-1.085%). The 1.643% spread is driven by a sharp divergence in macro data: UK inflation resilience allows GBP to shrug off disinflation headlines, whereas Australia's flash PMI slumped to 47.8 (contraction). Despite a -60bp OIS carry deficit for GBP/USD, the growth-momentum gap favors GBP over AUD. Watch for a break of the 1.9000 psychological level if AUD PMI weakness persists.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs heavily fading the kiwi) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the May 27 meeting. With NZD long% at an extreme low of 22.3%, a hawkish RBNZ delivery or signal creates significant short-squeeze risk toward 0.6000. NZD/USD at 0.5859 remains supported by improving market sentiment despite the 150bp Fed-RBNZ carry disadvantage. Watch the May 27 RBNZ decision for the squeeze catalyst.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7118 — COT LF net long +55,794 (74.8% long) contradicts retail sentiment at 69% short. This institutional vs retail divergence occurs amid an extreme Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +16.6% (IV 25.7% vs HV30 9.1%), signaling the options market is pricing in massive tail-risk protection. The combination of crowded institutional longs and extreme retail fading suggests a high-volatility liquidation event if price fails to hold 0.7100. Watch for retail capitulation or LF long reduction.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.93 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite a correlation break with VIX (z=+1.55σ). JPY's safe-haven function is currently broken; the pair is not unwinding on equity volatility as carry remains the dominant regime driver. However, 25d RR at -1.44 (moderate put skew) indicates the options market is paying for protection against a sharp JPY reversal, likely tied to BoJ intervention risk as price tests 160.00. Carry dominates until VIX > 25 or BoJ action.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Vol Premium + Macro Bottoming
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1624 — IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 5.0% generates a +2.1% VRP, signaling elevated hedging demand as the pair attempts to bottom. While ECB sources suggest a June hike is certain, the 175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage keeps upside structurally limited. Speculative positioning remains net short (lev_net -8,151), suggesting the recent rebound is a relief move on Iran-US talks rather than a trend reversal. Watch the 1.1700 resistance level and June 11 ECB meeting.
